message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German General Social Survey (AGB) – 1970","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1120","justification":"Wave 1970 contains detailed questions on political participation, party identification and perceptions of the German federal and state governments."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (AGB) – 1974","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1136","justification":"Wave 1974 provides data on attitudes toward political institutions, the Bundestag, and the political system of West Germany."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (AGB) – 1976","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1152","justification":"Wave 1976 captures respondents’ views on government policy, federal-state relations, and the structure of political parties in the 1970s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (AGB) – 1979","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1180","justification":"Final 1970s wave includes questions on the role of the German federal government, regional administrations, and political organization during the decade."},{"title":"German Federal Election Survey – 1972","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1150","justification":"Provides electoral data, voter attitudes, and party organization information for the 1972 Bundestag election."},{"title":"German Federal Election Survey – 1976","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1153","justification":"Includes data on political party structures, campaign strategies, and voter behavior during the 1976 federal election."},{"title":"German Social Survey – Political Attitudes 1970s Summary","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.1200","justification":"Summarises key findings on government perception, political participation, and organisational structures from 1970-1979 surveys."}]}
